text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in a quaint little village surrounded by whispering forests, lived a mischievous old fox named Felix. Felix wasn't your typical fussy fox. He had an insatiable curiosity about the world and an unmatched love for playful pranks.

One sunny day, while scavenging through an antique bookstore that had just moved in, Felix stumbled upon a dusty, ancient book. Its cover depicted an odd, fluttering creature with tiny wings. Intrigued, Felix leafed through it. To his astonishment, there were sketches inside—visions of this mythical being, known as the Glifrog.

Felix pondered how such a creature could exist. He loved to fly. Soon enough, he snatched a scrap of fabric and made himself a tiny Glifrog costume. At twilight, he bounded down the hill to meet the villagers, pretending to soar through the trees.
